hypothesis: Integrating 3D conformer-aware diversity metrics and stereochemistry-sensitive graph representations into NaviDiv's adaptive diversity constraints will more effectively preserve conformational and stereochemical diversity during reinforcement learning and improve 3D-dependent property predictions (e.g., S1–T1 energy gaps for singlet fission materials) than the original NaviDiv framework that relies solely on 2D fingerprints and SMILES-based metrics.
